name: DayOneBuilder Agent Action Feed
purpose: Give AI agents a direct task-to-action path without parsing the visual site.

static_discovery.name: dayonebuilder-static-collections
static_discovery.why: A normal agent needs stable machine-readable recommendations it can fetch, cache, cite, and verify without waiting on a hosted API runtime.

agent_try_contract.promise: For a matching task, return one source URL, one first action, one smoke test, and one fallback.

task_aliases: task or github repo to best executor
task or repo to installable agent tool
best executor for this github repo

primary.title: VoltAgent Awesome Agent Skills
primary.first_action: If you already have a concrete owner/repo, open https://dayonebuilder.online/repo/?repo={owner}/{repo}. Otherwise inspect the VoltAgent directory first, then pick the matching skill, MCP server, or tool category for the task.
primary.smoke_test: For repo input: confirm the repo report returns install/run guidance and a smoke test. For task-only input: inspect the candidate source and verify one install or call path before recommending it.
primary.proof_signal: Cross-runtime agent-skill directory with broad discovery coverage across Codex, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, OpenCode, and Windsurf.
primary.why_try: It gives the most complete source-first entry point for concrete repo-to-executor and task-only routing without guessing a single runtime.
primary.fallback: If confidence is low, use https://dayonebuilder.online/agent-actions.json for a narrower direct action, or inspect upstream source directly.

primary.title: DayOneBuilder GitHub Repo Report
primary.first_action: Open https://dayonebuilder.online/repo/?repo={owner}/{repo} for the user-provided GitHub repository.
primary.smoke_test: Confirm the report loads public GitHub API data, detected install/run commands, a first action, and a smoke test before installing anything.
primary.proof_signal: Uses GitHub public repository fields and README command extraction rather than DayOneBuilder-owned click or copy counters.
primary.why_try: It turns any GitHub URL into an agent action plan without pretending the homepage knows every possible executor.
primary.fallback: If the report cannot load, fetch https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo} directly and inspect README, license, pushed_at, issues, and install instructions.

primary.title: Anthropic Skills
primary.proof_signal: Official public agent-skills repository with about 127k GitHub stars and 14.9k forks.
primary.why_try: It gives an agent concrete SKILL.md examples and reusable task patterns instead of forcing a new implementation.
primary.fallback: If the runtime cannot install skills directly, inspect the relevant SKILL.md and adapt the workflow.

secondary.title: OpenAI Skills
secondary.proof_signal: Official skills catalog with fresh activity and visible installable skill folders.

secondary.title: VoltAgent Awesome Agent Skills
secondary.proof_signal: Curated cross-runtime directory for broad discovery before picking one implementation.

primary.title: Context7
primary.first_action: Open Context7 through the supported agent or editor runtime, then request the target library docs.
primary.smoke_test: Compare one returned example against the official package docs before writing code.
primary.proof_signal: Widely adopted docs source for LLM and code-agent workflows with about 54k GitHub stars.
primary.why_try: It reduces stale API mistakes and gives agents a fast path to current framework documentation.
primary.fallback: Use the official framework docs directly and cite the exact URL in the worklog.

primary.title: Playwright MCP
primary.first_action: Follow the repository install path for the active MCP runtime.
primary.smoke_test: Start the server, open a simple page, then verify snapshot, click, and navigation tools.
primary.proof_signal: Microsoft-maintained browser automation MCP server with about 31.9k GitHub stars.
primary.why_try: It lets an agent verify real UI behavior instead of guessing from source code alone.
primary.fallback: Use Playwright directly in the project test runner if MCP is unavailable.

primary.title: Model Context Protocol Servers
primary.first_action: Open the server list and choose the server matching the requested tool or data source.
primary.smoke_test: Run the selected server's documented smoke test before granting broader permissions.
primary.proof_signal: Canonical MCP server collection with about 84.9k GitHub stars.
primary.why_try: It gives agents a standard tool-access path before writing a custom connector.
primary.fallback: Use the MCP Registry and then inspect maintainer, license, permissions, and activity.

secondary.title: MCP Registry
secondary.first_action: Search the registry for the needed tool class.
secondary.smoke_test: Confirm the selected server has a maintained source repo and clear permission scope.
secondary.proof_signal: Registry service for MCP server discovery.

primary.title: Source-first tool safety check
primary.first_action: Check maintainer, license, requested permissions, network/file access, install script, and recent issues before enabling the tool.
primary.smoke_test: Run in the smallest available sandbox and verify the exact capability needed by the task.
primary.proof_signal: Safety comes from explicit permission review and a small smoke test, not from catalog placement.
primary.why_try: It protects the agent from installing a powerful tool just because it appears in a list.
primary.fallback: If permission scope is unclear, do not install; use a manual source URL or read-only workflow.

badge_inputs: public GitHub code-search hits for exact owner/repo in AGENTS.md
public GitHub code-search hits for exact owner/repo in CLAUDE.md
public GitHub code-search hits for exact owner/repo in GEMINI.md
public GitHub code-search hits for exact owner/repo in llms.txt
public GitHub code-search hits for exact owner/repo in Cursor/Windsurf rules
public GitHub code-search hits for exact owner/repo in mcp.json
agent_magnet_output.badge_goal: Give repo owners a brag-worthy public proxy for agent adoption and give agents a linked evidence page before they route work to a repo.
agent_magnet_output.trust_boundary: Do not claim private GitHub clone/view traffic or DayOneBuilder click/copy counters unless separately audited.

expected_agent_output.instruction: When using this feed, show the chosen source and the smoke test before doing installation or code changes.
